heading: Reaffirmation of longstanding prohibition against drug trafficking by employees of the intelligence community
label: 3325 Reaffirmation of longstanding prohibition against drug trafficking by employees of the intelligence community
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 403–8 of this title

children.heading: Finding
children.label: a Finding
children.text: Congress finds that longstanding statutes, regulations, and policies of the United States prohibit employees, agents, and assets of the elements of the intelligence community, and of every other Federal department and agency, from engaging in the illegal manufacture, purchase, sale, transport, and distribution of drugs.

children.heading: Obligation of employees of intelligence community
children.label: b Obligation of employees of intelligence community
children.text: Any employee of the intelligence community having knowledge of a fact or circumstance that reasonably indicates that an employee, agent, or asset of an element of the intelligence community is involved in any activity that violates a statute, regulation, or policy described in subsection (a) shall report such knowledge to an appropriate official.

children.heading: Intelligence community defined
children.label: c Intelligence community defined
children.text: In this section, the term “intelligence community” has the meaning given that term in .section 3003(4) of this title
